title: Exorcist: The Beginning
plot: Archeologist Lankester Merrin is asked to go to East Africa to excavate a church that has been found completely buried in sand. Merrin is also an ordained Roman Catholic priest who, still haunted by what he was forced to do during World War II in his native Holland, eschews any religion or belief. He's fascinated by what he finds and that it dates hundred of years before Christianity was introduced to the area. Accompanied by a young priest, Father Francis, to keep an eye on the religious elements of what they find, Merrin makes his way to the camp. There he meets a young doctor, Sarah and soon realizes there is an air of gloom that envelops the entire site. Workmen go mad and a young boy is mauled by a pack of hyenas while completely ignoring his younger brother Joseph. Inside the church itself they find signs of desecration. Merrin is forced to re-examine his lack of faith and come face to face with the devil.
actors: Stellan Skarsgård, Izabella Scorupco, James D'Arcy
production_country: United Kingdom, France, Italy, Pakistan, United States

categories.label: European experts claiming African landscapes as workplaces
categories.description: Doctors, archaeologists, engineers, and officials often treat African settings as fields for discovery, cure, and control, reflecting the unequal habits of late empire. What you'll see: excavation camps, hospital wards, survey gear, clinical authority, hired labour, and foreign specialists directing local space.

categories.label: church missions beside older sacred ground
categories.description: Christian missions and clergy are established presences, yet they stand in lands where older beliefs, local holy places, and spirit fears still shape how misfortune is understood. What you'll see: priests and vestments, mission stations, prayers over sickness and evil, excavated ruins, and villagers reading omens in the land.

title: Naked Lunch
plot: Not an adaptation of beat writer William S. Burrough's novel but a mix of biography and an interpretation of his drug- induced writing processes combined with elements of his work in this paranoid fantasy about Bill Lee, a writer who accidentally shoots his wife, whose typewriter transforms into a cockroach and who becomes involved in a mysterious plot in North African port called Interzone. Wonderfully bizarre, not unlike Burrough's books.
actors: Peter Weller, Judy Davis, Ian Holm

categories.label: port cities and desert crossroads full of transients
categories.description: North African and East African hubs gather smugglers, diplomats, pilgrims, soldiers, and drifters, making them places of constant passage and uncertain allegiance. What you'll see: harbours, railway stations, hotel lobbies, market stalls, desert roads, many languages in one street, and a bustle fed by arrivals and departures.

title: The Siege of Jadotville
plot: In 1961, the UN sends an Irish peacekeeper troop commanded by Commandant Pat Quinlan to Katanga, in Congo, to protect the inhabitants of the mining town of Jadotville in the beginning of a civil war. Meanwhile the UN advisor Dr. Conor Cruise O'Brien launches a military attack named Operation Morthor against the French and Belgian mercenaries. Soon there is a reprisal from the mercenaries and Quinlan and his men are left under siege by a huge number of Katangese and mercenary troops. Will the Irish soldiers resist the attack?
actors: Jamie Dornan, Emmanuelle Seigner, Mark Strong

categories.label: soldiers, mercenaries, and peacekeepers in the same war
categories.description: Armed force is no longer only imperial armies facing rebels; it also includes United Nations troops, hired European fighters, and hastily assembled local forces. What you'll see: mixed uniforms, roadblocks, sandbagged compounds, foreign accents in the ranks, and battles fought amid civilian streets and mine roads.

categories.label: cold war powers working through local crises
categories.description: African cities and provinces become places where outside powers test influence, trade favours, and back rivals without always declaring themselves openly. What you'll see: intelligence men under cover, coded meetings in hotels and clubs, foreign advisers, and local struggles watched through a world-power lens.

title: Breathe
plot: When Robin (Andrew Garfield) is struck down by polio at the age of twenty-eight, he is confined to a hospital bed and given only a few months to live. With the help of Diana's (Claire Foy's) twin brothers (Tom Hollander) and the groundbreaking ideas of inventor Teddy Hall (Hugh Bonneville), Robin and Diana dare to escape the hospital ward to seek out a full and passionate life together, raising their young son, travelling, and devoting their lives to helping other polio patients.
actors: Andrew Garfield, Claire Foy, Hugh Bonneville

categories.label: new states strained by fragile authority
categories.description: Independence does not instantly bring settled rule, and governments often inherit weak institutions, divided loyalties, and contested borders. What you'll see: hurried official decrees, nervous ministers, improvised security measures, and uncertainty over who truly commands a town, province, or garrison.

title: OSS 117: Cairo, Nest of Spies
plot: An homage to classic spy films. It's 1955 and after a fellow agent and close friend disappears, secret agent Hubert Bonisseur de la Bath, a.k.a. OSS 117, is ordered to take his place at the head of a poultry firm in Cairo. This is to be his cover while he is busy investigating, foiling Nazi holdouts, quelling a fundamentalist rebellion, and bedding local beauties.
actors: François Damiens, Jean Dujardin, Khalid Maadour

categories.label: cold war powers working through local crises
categories.description: African cities and provinces become places where outside powers test influence, trade favours, and back rivals without always declaring themselves openly. What you'll see: intelligence men under cover, coded meetings in hotels and clubs, foreign advisers, and local struggles watched through a world-power lens.

categories.label: port cities and desert crossroads full of transients
categories.description: North African and East African hubs gather smugglers, diplomats, pilgrims, soldiers, and drifters, making them places of constant passage and uncertain allegiance. What you'll see: harbours, railway stations, hotel lobbies, market stalls, desert roads, many languages in one street, and a bustle fed by arrivals and departures.

title: The Battle of Algiers
plot: A film commissioned by the Algerian government that shows the Algerian revolution from both sides. The French foreign legion has left Vietnam in defeat and has something to prove. The Algerians are seeking independence. The two clash. The torture used by the French is contrasted with the Algerian's use of bombs in soda shops. A look at war as a nasty thing that harms and sullies everyone who participates in it.
actors: Brahim Hadjadj, Jean Martin, Yacef Saadi

categories.label: colonial rule giving way to independence
categories.description: Across much of the continent, European empires are losing their hold, but they still command armies, police, and administrations that do not yield without force. What you'll see: checkpoints, curfews, tricolours and imperial insignia, nationalist crowds, and streets where old authority and new claims collide.

categories.label: public order maintained through raids and torture
categories.description: States facing revolt rely on interrogation, sweeps, and exemplary violence, presenting coercion as necessary to restore peace. What you'll see: night arrests, military patrols, crowded cells, interrogators at work, and neighbourhoods living under fear of the next knock or cordon.

title: I'm for the Hippopotamus
plot: In Africa, many years ago, Slim and Tom don't like it when a German tyrant, starts selling all of the African wildlife to Canadian Zoo's! Slim and Tom must teach this guy a lesson by beating the hell out of him and his gang, left and right!
actors: Terence Hill, Bud Spencer, Joe Bugner

categories.label: mines and trade routes drawing foreign hands
categories.description: Copper belts, ports, and transport lines keep outside money and outside interference tied tightly to African ground. What you'll see: company towns, airstrips and rail links, guarded warehouses, expatriate compounds, and disputes that shadow the movement of ore, goods, and cash.
